title: Beyond Words
anchor_summary: Single-player roguelite word-board puzzle/strategy game where you form words on changing boards, stack cards/modifiers/perks, and chase high-scoring synergies through boss-twisted runs.

similar_games.title: Letter Quest: Grimm's Journey Remastered
similar_games.why_similar: Word-building on a letter grid is the core action, with run-shaping upgrades, hazards, and optimization around scoring and survival.

similar_games.title: Bookworm Adventures Deluxe
similar_games.why_similar: Builds progression around spelling words from letter tiles, rewarding vocabulary, special tile use, and tactical planning over action execution.

similar_games.title: Super SpellTower
similar_games.why_similar: A board-centric word puzzle focused on tile management, future-turn planning, and score-maximizing clears rather than narrative or character control.

similar_games.title: Balatro
similar_games.why_similar: Shares the roguelite score-chasing structure: assemble broken synergies from modifiers, adapt to boss rule twists, and optimize each run for explosive scoring.

similar_games.title: Luck be a Landlord
similar_games.why_similar: Another abstract roguelite built around manipulating a pool of symbols and compounding synergies to hit escalating score targets across repeat runs.

research_payload.evidence_summary: Identity is well disambiguated by the provided Steam app ID, release date, developer/publisher, and matching external pages. Official Steam and PQube pages are highly detailed and internally consistent: Beyond Words is a single-player, run-based word-crafting strategy/puzzle game where players place words on changing boards, manipulate letter values and tiles, assemble synergies from cards/modifiers/perks, and clear boss-style rule twists. Metacritic aligns on title, release date, developer, publisher, platforms, and logic-puzzle framing.

core_loop: Form words on a board using letter tiles to score and progress through a run.
Optimize placements for combos, multipliers, pickups, and future turns rather than just making any valid word.
Manipulate the tile pool and scoring rules by changing letter scores, duplicating favored letters, destroying unwanted letters, and stacking modifiers/stickers/enhancements.
Adapt to shifting board layouts, rules, and objectives while building stronger scoring synergies over the course of a run.
Overcome boss encounters that alter the rules of play, then push into harder challenge variants for higher scores and mastery.
perspective: Abstract 2D board-and-tile presentation centered on letter placement rather than character control.
Gameplay is framed around boards, tile types, layouts, and word positioning, indicating a puzzle-board playfield view.
combat: There is no conventional action or character combat.
Opposition is expressed through boss challenges and board obstacles that twist rules, invert scoring, limit word length, scramble the board, or block/open parts of the board.
Success comes from planning, vocabulary, placement, and combo optimization rather than attacking enemies in real time.
progression: Runs are shaped by unlocking or acquiring game-changing upgrades and an expanding toolbox of options.
Buildcraft comes from combining 300+ modifiers and abilities, including supercharged tile types, booster cards, power cards, pickups, and perks.
Players can build and enhance the tile deck for better opportunities and payoffs.
Power cards reshape rules of play and can change scoring, effects, or board behavior.
Perks provide passive bonuses that define long-term strategy within a run, while shop upgrades add further run-shaping decisions.
structure: Run-based roguelike structure with escalating targets and replay-oriented challenge.
Each board is presented as a unique puzzle with changing layouts, rules, and objectives.
Bosses appear every few stages, creating milestone encounters inside a run.
The game emphasizes repeat runs, synergies, and mastery through endless combinations and endless play.
modes: A negative-scoring challenge variant described as the 'flipside of negative scoring'.
dlc_relationship: Official/store materials reviewed present Beyond Words as a standalone base game.
No DLC or expansion relationship was indicated on the official Steam or PQube pages reviewed.

source_notes: Steam store page provided the clearest gameplay summary and feature set: single-player, word combos, upgrades, modifiers, time-attack boards, boss challenges, seeded runs, negative scoring, and endless play.

PQube's official game page materially strengthened evidence with concrete subsystem details: power cards, booster cards, perks, tile deck building, board quirks, discards, pickups, shop upgrades, and bosses appearing every few stages.
Metacritic was used mainly for identity confirmation and cross-source consistency on release date, developer, publisher, platforms, and logic-puzzle framing.
Official first-party sources were sufficiently detailed and consistent to support strong evidence quality without needing extensive reliance on third-party reviews.

Persistent cross-run metaprogression is not described with enough precision in the reviewed sources, so progression facts are stated conservatively as run-shaping upgrades/buildcraft rather than confirmed permanent unlock trees.

title: The Occultist
anchor_summary: First-person single-player narrative horror built around exploration, puzzle-solving, occult investigation, and stealthy avoidance of supernatural threats rather than direct combat.

similar_games.title: Amnesia: The Dark Descent
similar_games.why_similar: First-person survival horror focused on exploration, environmental puzzles, and hiding from threats instead of fighting them.

similar_games.title: Outlast
similar_games.why_similar: A linear first-person horror game centered on investigation, stealth, chase sequences, and total combat avoidance.

similar_games.title: SOMA
similar_games.why_similar: Story-driven first-person horror with puzzle gates, environmental storytelling, and stealth around dangerous enemies.

similar_games.title: Maid of Sker
similar_games.why_similar: First-person survival horror emphasizing stealth, exploration, puzzle progression, and evasion of hostile cult-linked creatures.

similar_games.title: Visage
similar_games.why_similar: First-person psychological horror built around tense exploration, supernatural encounters, and puzzle-led progression without action-heavy combat.

research_payload.evidence_summary: Strong first-party/store evidence consistently identifies The Occultist as a first-person, single-player narrative horror/survival-horror game focused on investigation, exploration, pendulum-based environmental interaction, stealth avoidance instead of direct combat, and puzzle-solving on the deserted island of Godstone. Official materials are internally consistent across Steam, PlayStation, Xbox, DALOAR, and Daedalic press materials.

core_loop: Explore the abandoned island of Godstone as paranormal investigator Alan Rebels while searching for the truth behind his father's disappearance.
Use Alan's mystic pendulum to interact with and modify the environment through four described mechanics.
Investigate occult phenomena, confront supernatural threats, and solve puzzles to keep advancing the story.
perspective: Official store and developer materials describe the game as first-person.
The game is framed as a narrative thriller / narrative horror experience played through Alan Rebels' eyes.
combat: Official feature lists state that Alan avoids direct combat.
Threat management is built around stealth, hiding, sneaking, and avoiding hostile supernatural entities rather than fighting them head-on.
Enemies are presented as spectral, monstrous, or supernatural adversaries that create danger during exploration and puzzle sequences.
progression: Progression is story-driven, centered on unraveling Godstone's cult history and Alan's family mystery.
Advancement is tied to exploration, pendulum-based interaction, stealth survival, and puzzle-solving.
The consulted official/store sources do not describe class-based builds or weapon-upgrade progression; they emphasize narrative and mechanic-gated advancement instead.
structure: The game takes place on the foggy British island of Godstone, with exploration across streets, buildings, and other abandoned locations.
Its structure is presented as narrative-driven horror investigation with exploration, stealth sections, supernatural encounters, and puzzle gates.
Official descriptions emphasize a contained survival-horror/thriller journey rather than multiplayer, sandbox, or mission-arena structure.
dlc_relationship: Official store pages list a Deluxe Edition / Deluxe Upgrade Pack, but the described extras are non-gameplay bonuses such as the soundtrack and artbook.
A separate soundtrack product is listed on Steam; no gameplay-expansion DLC was identified in the consulted official store materials.

source_notes: Steam, PlayStation Store, and Xbox Store provide near-identical official feature lists covering first-person play, pendulum interaction, survival-horror framing, stealth/no direct combat, and puzzles.
The DALOAR presskit confirms the protagonist, setting, and first-person narrative-thriller identity from the developer side.
Daedalic/Games Press trailer messaging adds corroboration for pendulum powers, puzzle encounters, and spectral/monstrous enemies.
Official store evidence is detailed enough to support a strong evidence rating even without relying on review-text coverage.
